subject: Mathematics
title: Lines and Angles
description: A quick win: understand Lines and Angles.

hook.text: A line stretches infinitely in both directions. It has length but no width.

buildup.text: When two lines meet, they form an angle. A right angle is exactly 90 degrees.

discovery.text: Acute angles are less than 90. Obtuse angles are more. Straight angles are exactly 180.

twist.text: Angles aren't just abstract. Every corner of every building is an angle made real.

climax.text: Understanding angles unlocks trigonometry, architecture, navigation, and engineering.

punchline.text: Two lines meet, and the space between them measures the world.

quiz.question: What is an angle greater than 90 degrees but less than 180 called?
